en: 49th, 51st, 52nd, 54th, 56th, 57th, 58th or 60th day of the sexagenary cycle (said to be inauspicious with a high probability of rain)

sound of something being powerfully ejected
the eight gods who preside over the lucky/unlucky directions of the traditional calendar for each year (in Onmyōdō)

god of fortune in Onmyōdō who descends to the northeast on the 46th day of the sexagenary cycle and completes a clockwise circuit, spending five days on each cardinal point and six days on each ordinal point, returning to heaven from the north on the 30th day of the next sexagenary cycle; travelling in the direction of Ten'ichijin is considered unlucky
pt: ['Ten’ichijin', 'Nakagami', 'divindade da fortuna no Onmyōdō que desce para o nordeste no 46º dia do ciclo sexagenário e completa um circuito no sentido horário, permanecendo cinco dias em cada ponto cardeal e seis dias em cada ponto colateral, retornando ao céu pelo norte no 30º dia do ciclo sexagenário seguinte; viajar na direção de Ten’ichijin é considerado azar']
